heading: Identification of, and imposition of measures with respect to, foreign government agencies carrying out activities or transactions with certain Iran-affiliated persons
label: 8743 Identification of, and imposition of measures with respect to, foreign government agencies carrying out activities or transactions with certain Iran-affiliated persons
notes: Termination of SectionFor termination of section, see .section 8785(a) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 120 days after , and every 180 days thereafter, the President shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report that identifies each agency of the government of a foreign country (other than Iran) that the President determines knowingly and materially assisted, sponsored, or provided financial, material, or technological support for, or goods or services in support of, or knowingly and materially engaged in a significant transaction with, any person described in paragraph (2).August 10, 2012

children.label: A
children.text: a foreign person that is an official, agent, or affiliate of Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps that is designated for the imposition of sanctions pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.);50 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: i
children.text: the Annex of United Nations Security Council Resolution 1737 (2006);

children.label: ii
children.text: Annex I of United Nations Security Council Resolution 1747 (2007);

children.label: iii
children.text: Annex I, II, or III of United Nations Security Council Resolution 1803 (2008);

children.label: iv
children.text: Annex I, II, or III of United Nations Security Council Resolution 1929 (2010); or

children.label: v
children.text: any subsequent and related United Nations Security Council resolution, or any annex thereto, that imposes new sanctions with respect to Iran or modifies existing sanctions with respect to Iran; or

children.label: C
children.text: a foreign person that the agency knows is acting on behalf of or at the direction of, or owned or controlled by, a person described in subparagraph (A) or (B).

children.heading: Form of report
children.label: 3 Form of report
children.text: Each report submitted under paragraph (1) shall be submitted in unclassified form but may contain a classified annex.

children.label: A
children.text: No assistance may be provided to the agency under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.) or the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.) other than humanitarian assistance or the provision of food or other agricultural commodities.22 U.S.C. 215122 U.S.C. 2751

children.label: B
children.text: No sales of any defense articles, defense services, or design and construction services under the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.) may be made to the agency.22 U.S.C. 2751

children.label: C
children.text: No licenses for export of any item on the United States Munitions List that include the agency as a party to the license may be granted.

children.label: D
children.text: No exports may be permitted to the agency of any goods or technologies controlled for national security reasons under the Export Administration Regulations, except that such prohibition shall not apply to any transaction subject to the reporting requirements of title V of the National Security Act of 1947 ([ et seq.]; relating to congressional oversight of intelligence activities).50 U.S.C. 3091

children.label: E
children.text: The United States shall oppose any loan or financial or technical assistance to the agency by international financial institutions in accordance with .section 262d of this title

children.label: i
children.text: to any transaction subject to the reporting requirements of title V of the National Security Act of 1947 ([ et seq.]; relating to congressional oversight of intelligence activities);50 U.S.C. 3091

children.label: ii
children.text: to the provision of medicines, medical equipment, and humanitarian assistance; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to any credit, credit guarantee, or financial assistance provided by the Department of Agriculture to support the purchase of food or other agricultural commodities.

children.label: G
children.text: Additional restrictions as may be imposed pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.).50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 2 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to impose measures with respect to programs under section 1501 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1997 ( note) and programs under the Atomic Energy Defense Act ( et seq.).50 U.S.C. 2632150 U.S.C. 25012So in original. Probably should be “2362”.1See References in Text note below.2

children.label: A
children.text: a person described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (a)(2) with respect to which the agency is carrying out activities or transactions is no longer designated pursuant to subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (a)(2); or

children.label: B
children.text: any person described in subparagraph (C) of subsection (a)(2) with respect to which the agency is carrying out activities or transactions is no longer acting on behalf of or at the direction of, or owned or controlled by, any person described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (a)(2);

the agency is no longer carrying out activities or transactions for which the measures were imposed and has provided assurances to the United States Government that the agency will not carry out the activities or transactions in the future; or
it is essential to the national security interest of the United States to terminate such measures.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: d Waiver
children.text: If the President does not impose one or more measures described in subsection (b) with respect to an agency identified in the report required by subsection (a), the President shall include in the subsequent report an explanation as to why the President did not impose such measures.

the Committee on Foreign Relations, the Committee on Appropriations, the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs, the Committee on Finance, and the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate; and

the Committee on Foreign Affairs, the Committee on Appropriations, the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Financial Services, the Committee on Ways and Means, and the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives.
the date that is 45 days after ; orAugust 10, 2012
the date that is 45 days after a person is designated as described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (a)(2).
